title: Positron Rifle issue
wikitext:
[]: Positron Rifle issue             Quote Post by Tier_15 » Tue Feb 18, 2014 4:42 am Broke it into paragraphs because it was very hard to read as a wall of text. Please use paragraphs for your next long post. - Ursus hello. I've been watching the show since I was 14 back in 1999. The first episode I ever saw was episode seven. Being a big-time military weapons fanatic even back then, I was rather perplexed as the show progressed on due to a number of questions regarding the methodologies of fighting the Angels. I understand this is a show and that there is a suspension of disbelief but I am quite a stickler and the show was appealing to me in that it actually tried to be realistic unlike many of the other shows within the giant robot genre. The question that always bothered me is that in dealing with a defensive system such as that created by the Angels in the form of the AT field there are far simpler ways to counter the threat then essentially reverse engineered "angel" or a nuclear power giant robot (jetalone). despite the seemingly advanced nature of the Angels the fact that it is even possible to kill them does show that behind their defense protection systems they are actually quite vulnerable. It made sense to utilize a very high-powered collimated beam of energy targeted at a very small point of the at field in order to overwhelm it and penetrate through. This is the basis of many different weapons systems and anyone who's played world of tanks understands that penetration is key since the crew that is behind the armor is far more vulnerable but you just have to get a round past the armor . A weapon system consisting of a extremely capable self-propelled positron gun would make far more sense than the construction of a biomechanical copy of a being that exhibits near godlike qualities. This is a ignoring the other motives for the construction of the E units but it is the one hole in the story that has always bothered me. I would think there would be far more conventional means to try and defeat such beings than resorting to a very complex undertaking to essentially copy them without fully understanding how exactly everything works. This was answered to some degree in the rebuilds where it was stated that it was to be part of a self-propelled gun, the positron rifle, but it systems had not been completed in time which is why unit 01 had to operate it in a manner similar to a conventional sniper rifle. It just seems that completing a system like that would be far easier and take up much less resources than trying to go through all the crazy things that occurred with the evangelion project. If I was in charge of Tokyo 3 I would've likely installed a large number of high-capacity molten salt nuclear reactors that would power a array of self-propelled guns which would be very advanced versions of the prototype used in that episode. Taking all the money I would save from canceling the e project I would think I could build an extremely advanced gun system that would prove to be a very capable defensive system. This wouldn't work on all of the Angels but it does seem as a more practical starting point than reverse engineering an alien being. I am aware I am completely ignoring the motives of gendo and SEELE which in all honesty it would be possible in the real world for the motivations of those in high power positions to circumvent practical thought. This has occurred many times in weapon developmental projects where in the worst cases a horrible product is not only created but actually used. This is part of the problem of having speech recognition software, I can put a lot of thoughts down so I figured I might as well and see what you guys think. I apologize by sound like I'm rambling

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue Feb 18, 2014 4:59 am Okay, first thing : AERATE YOUR TEXT! It's really an hassle to finish a wall of text like his one, use line breaks! Now for your question, the Positron has three big flaws that make it impossible to use as a regular defense system : 1/ To be able to pierce an AT Field, it needs an astronomic amount of energy : to pierce Ramiel's AT Field they needed the energy from all of Japan. And that's not just because the Postron Rifle was a prototype, that's really the amount of power needed to pierce an AT Field. Which also lead to the second problem. 2/ It takes too much time in the initial charge up. Ramiel was a static target, that's why the rifle could take the various minutes it took to completely charge and shoot it. And once completely charged it could chain the shoots yes, but that still leave to the attacking Angel a good five minutes before the Rifle had the time to begin to shoot, time enough for the Angel to detect the massive amount of building energy and shoot it with an eyebeam, and because of the gigantic amount of energy needed(all of Japan I recall), there can't be more than one rifle active at the same time. 3/ It wouldn't be useful against all the Angels : sure it could be good against Sachiel, Shamshell, Ramiel or Matarael, but how do it stops the ones that don't play by the rules, like Leliel who's a living shadow, or Sahaquiel and Arael who can attack from orbit?(and they did try to shoot Arael with the Positron Rifle, but he was out of the rifle's range, that's why it could attack with total impunity) The Evangelions have their flaws for sure, they are unstable and not completely reliable, but they have qualities that a Positron Rifle don't have which are vitals in the Angel War : they can be deployed quickly, they are very resistant and they can adapt to the situation, things that a mobile Positron Rifle can't.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Tue Feb 18, 2014 8:05 am Even if they solved the energy problem, how would they deal with Angels that didn't take the form of giant monsters? Leliel was bizarre, and Ireul and Bardiel were colonies of microorganisms. They wouldn't have been targets for a positron rifle system! And how do you protect the entire gun system against increasingly powerful and weird Angels? Also remember, the Evas had a doomsday capability as well. They weren't primarily meant to be weapons systems, although they were highly effective in the role. An Eva with an S2 engine (providing theoretically infinite energy, the same as the Angels) would have been able to deal with the Angel threat and possibly even evolve against a weapon the positron rifle battery would be incapable of dealing with. In the real world, the system you mention would have made absolute sense. But with a world with such weirdo monsters and bizzarre science, the sensible conventional answer is usually written out.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue Feb 18, 2014 12:46 pm OP: For the same reason that the government in Pacific Rim didn't just place a ton of those blue lazer-energy cannons around the breach: EVERYTHING looks cooler on/around/controlled by giant mecha thingies.

[]:             Quote Post by user-02 » Wed Feb 19, 2014 12:19 am ElMariachi wrote: and they did try to shoot Arael with the Positron Rifle, but he was out of the rifle's range, why it could attack with total impunity

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Feb 19, 2014 2:04 am Effective range will depend on beam collimation. As the beam spreads with distance, the flux density will drop.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Wed Feb 19, 2014 3:28 am @ user-02 : They needed to draw all the power of Japan to fire that monster. Also, I don't know shit about the theory behind that particle beam, but maybe passing through the atmosphere weakened the beam enough to make it easily stoppable by Arael's AT Field? But anyway, the out of range thing was just a plot excuse to use the Spear of Longinus.

[]:             Quote Post by Tier_15 » Wed Feb 19, 2014 5:30 am Sorry about not breaking my paragraphs. I wrote this at 3am since I could not sleep so I guess it was the equal of a drunk dial LOL. Pacific Rim was what got me thinking about this. I just couldn't get through it without just throwing my hands up and walking away saying "What...How can you.....why would they......what the....so why are we calling 300 helicopters? and OMG its Independence day". I guess my natural tendencies made me wonder if there is a cheaper and more efficient way to kill angels. Has any1 been able to put a price on the AAA Wunder? I mean it seems like there had to be other options rather than reverse engineering. Why would the FAR even make something like an angel to begin with? LOL 15 years of watching EVA and I'm still trying to figure this stuff out.... Targeting issues and all that could be true depending on the angel. The power issue puzzles me because it quantifies the energy needed to pierce the field which then means that the EVAs are not the only weapon system capable though it helps that they can generate their own AT field and neutralize the other without putting Japan in a blackout. LOL its not "NERV or nothing" if you know info like that. Well tanks again for your opinions and info.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Wed Feb 19, 2014 5:55 am In the end, it all boils down to one thing : AT Fields. An AT Field generated by an Angel is so strong than the means to break it are so resource costing that using something that can generate its own AT Fiend to erode the Angel's one. Once the AT Field is destroyed most of them can be destroyed with very conventional methods : Matarael got killed by a sneak attack with a simple Pallet Rifle after all. Of course, that's without counting the extremely strong ones like Zeruel or the physic defying ones like Leliel or Ireul. As for the Wunder, well first we don't even know who built that thing, the fact Mark.09 was it's original master could mean that SEELE are the ones who built it and that it was stolen by WILLE later. But in any case it probably cost more than the entire fleet accompanying it and it's two Evangelions. But the advantages compensate by far : it's mobile, sturdy, have all the facilities to maintain Evangelionsand once a suitable power source is found it can fly and make fly its fleet(increasing exponentially the projection capabilities of the Evangelions) and has it own AT Field, meaning that it can survive an attack by Angels or Evas(NS included) In short, in the spirit the Wunder is an upgraded and mobile version of Tokyo-3 which can actually be useful in battle and deploy the Evangelions virtually anywhere since they can come back to it for resupplying in equipment and recharge pods. And since the war against SEELE is an offensive war against an armored fortress(neo-NERV HQ) instead of a defensive one(protecting Lilith against the Angels), something like the Wunder is of vital importance for WILLE to win the war.
